resources.string: • A triangle has three sides and angles. \n • Includes vertices, sides, and special lines. \n • Triangles differ by sides, angles, and congruence.
• Every triangle has bisectors, medians, and altitudes. \n • Larger sides face larger angles. \n • The exterior angle is greater than any interior one.
• Base angles in isosceles triangles are equal. \n • The median to the base is also a bisector and altitude. \n • Only one perpendicular can be drawn from a point to a line.
• Geometric construction uses only a ruler and compass. \n • The ruler draws lines, the compass marks circles and segments. \n • Problems are solved through precise constructions.
• The ruler draws lines but doesn’t measure lengths. \n • The compass transfers distances between points. \n • Both tools define classic constructions.
• Triangle construction: Draw base and intersect circles. \n • Angle bisector: Use equal-radius arcs from the vertex. \n • Find intersection to draw bisecting ray.
• Parallel lines never meet. \n • A transversal cuts two lines at different points. \n • Creates alternate and corresponding angles.
• Through a point, one parallel can be drawn to a line. \n • Lines parallel to the same line are also parallel. \n • In a triangle, angles sum to 180°; polygon sums follow 180°×(n−2).
• Proved parallel lines by equal alternate angles. \n • Triangle angle sum shown using a parallel line. \n • Exterior angle helps confirm equality.
• Parallelogram has opposite sides parallel. \n • Rectangle, rhombus, and square are special cases. \n • Trapezoid has one pair of parallel sides.
• Parallelogram sides and angles are equal. \n • Rhombus diagonals are perpendicular and bisect angles. \n • Triangle and trapezoid midlines are parallel and half-lengths.
• Parallelogram sides proved equal via congruent triangles. \n • Thales theorem used to divide a segment equally. \n • Diagonal helps demonstrate equal relationships.
